title: 2010 Asahi Shimbun Russian seiyuu for 20
wikitext:
[]: 2010 Asahi Shimbun: Russian seiyuu for 2.0             Quote Post by gwern » Wed Aug 03, 2011 4:05 pm http://www.usagi.org/doi/seiyuu/books/newspapers/asahi20100512.html There is a 1/4 page article about Jenya on the front page of the Evening edition of the Asahi Shinbun Newspaper on May 12, 2010. The article tells about Jenya becoming a fan of Sailor Moon when she was in Russia, making her own home page and singing songs in Japanese, and then coming over to Japan in 2002 for a one week trip. Then she came over to live in Japan in 2005, and joined the seiyuu production Media Force in 2008. But it wasn't easy and she even worked at a maid cafe in Akihabara. Jenya also voiced the Russian lines in the 2009 Evangelion movie. So far Jenya has worked as a seiyuu in 5 anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Aug 03, 2011 4:56 pm Maybe I'm a slowpoke but I never heard anything about this girl before, there's an interview here . She's pretty amazing, imo.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Wed Aug 03, 2011 6:38 pm EVANGELION: Making dreams come true since 1997!

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Wed Aug 03, 2011 6:41 pm Shogo-Kun: we all make mistakes sometimes; I suppose that was bound to happen.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Aug 03, 2011 8:47 pm So was she was the Blonde Mari at the Russian NERV base?

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Thu Aug 04, 2011 1:44 am Probably either the voice of the PA system or one of the bridge bunnies.

[]: 2010 Asahi Shimbun: Russian seiyuu for 2.0             Quote Post by gwern » Wed Aug 03, 2011 4:05 pm http://www.usagi.org/doi/seiyuu/books/newspapers/asahi20100512.html There is a 1/4 page article about Jenya on the front page of the Evening edition of the Asahi Shinbun Newspaper on May 12, 2010. The article tells about Jenya becoming a fan of Sailor Moon when she was in Russia, making her own home page and singing songs in Japanese, and then coming over to Japan in 2002 for a one week trip. Then she came over to live in Japan in 2005, and joined the seiyuu production Media Force in 2008. But it wasn't easy and she even worked at a maid cafe in Akihabara. Jenya also voiced the Russian lines in the 2009 Evangelion movie. So far Jenya has worked as a seiyuu in 5 anime.
